intro: Punjuba callejasii, also known as Punilla, is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range in South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory infections. Punjuba callejasii contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Punjuba callejasii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, flu, and gastrointestinal disorders.
